title: Arco SEO help
description:
Hi seeking help w/ a client request please. 

Requests:
1. Camera Inspection Services – Keyword Optimization
  • After reviewing user queries, I noticed that most users search for “plumbing camera inspection” and “sewer camera inspection” instead of the generic “camera inspection.” Currently, we are ranking around positions 50–60 for these higher‑value terms. However, the service page does not include these specific keywords, which is a missed SEO opportunity. Updating the content to include the correct terminology could help us capture relevant search traffic and improve rankings. Please validate if we can optimize the page accordingly.

2. Missing Internal Linking 
  • Some plumbing-related pages still do not have internal links. For example, the air duct repair page currently has CTAs that are not linked.

3. Hide Empty Pages 
  • There are still empty pages appearing on the site, such as the one linked earlier. Please hide or remove such pages to avoid crawl issues.
    https://www.goarco.com/about-us/ladies/ 
 

Thanks much!

*the other items not inside the box are already assigned via dev team

title: ARCO CES Page Creation
description:
Seeking help with a client request please. The client gave us a heads up during the call earlier about a page they want. 

They would like to create an interactive experience on our website for a community engagement campaign. Here’s what we’re envisioning:

Project Overview:
  • Users scan a QR code from a printed coloring sheet and are taken to a dedicated landing page.
  • The landing page will feature an interactive digital coloring canvas with a pre-made otter outline.
  • Users can color the otter digitally, name their otter, and submit their creation.
  • Submissions (colored image + otter name) should be automatically collected in a Google Drive folder or Google Sheet. Ideally, we’d like the submissions to be easy to organize for a Google Slides gallery.
Requirements / Considerations:
  1. Mobile-friendly interface – many participants will be on phones or tablets.
  2. Simple, kid-friendly coloring tools (brush/fill bucket and color palette).
  3. Submission form should collect at least:
    • Otter Name
    • Parent Name / Email
    • Colored image
  4. Automatic saving of submissions to a shared folder/Sheet for internal review.
  5. Optional: The ability to automatically generate a slide for each submission in Google Slides.
 
Notes take from the call: 
  • this will serve like a community engagement campaign part of the site
  • they will have a qr code that can be scanned and will route to the page
  • it will an interactive coloring canvas where kids can color and mascot and name it
  • they will also need this posted on their GBP

*still getting the date and images to use


Thanks much!

title: Include Sitemap In footer of Client websites
assignees: Keith Bruss, Mary De Guzman, Predrag
description:
Hey Team!

To enhance our technical SEO baseline, improve crawl efficiency, and ensure consistent signaling across all client properties, It would be great to come to an agreement to outlining a new, standardized protocol for crawl management.

My suggestion is as follows:

Implement the following three directives on all client websites as part of our standard build and maintenance process.

1. XML Sitemap in robots.txt (Primary Directive)

This is the most effective way to inform search engines of all indexable URLs.

Action: Ensure the robots.txt file for every client domain contains a Sitemap directive pointing to the full URL of the XML sitemap.

Implementation Example:
User-agent: 
Sitemap: https://www.clientdomain.com/sitemap.xml

The "Why": This provides a clear, direct path for Google to find and crawl our most important pages, ensuring faster discovery and indexing of new content.

2. User-Facing HTML Sitemap (Footer)

This is for user experience and secondary crawl pathing.

Action: All client websites must have a user-facing HTML sitemap.

Implementation: This sitemap must be linked in the footer of every page on the site.

The "Why": This provides a clear navigation path for users, improves site accessibility, and   acts as a secondary crawl path for search engines to discover deep pages.

Strategic Crawl & Indexation Control

This is critical. We must be precise in telling crawlers what to crawl and what to index.

A. To Control Crawling (Using robots.txt)

We must Disallow crawlers from accessing low-value, non-SEO pages to conserve crawl budget.

Action: Audit and update the robots.txt file to Disallow non-public or utility sections of the site.
Implementation Examples:
Disallow: /wp-admin/

title: Primary Service Focus
description:
Hi I would like a little more context around the clients primary area of focus for this website. I have been through the site in its entirety and it is well balanced in terms of promoting all HVAC Services - except when we come to the Service Area Pages. 

Example: https://www.goarco.com/about-us/service-area/cleveland-heating-install-repair/

Every service area page is deeply focused on Heating only with no linking or mention of the other service silos. Is this by clients instructions?  Even though the brand covers Cooling, Plumbing, and Electrical, those silos aren’t reflected here — so Google sees these as heating-only local pages, not general HVAC hubs.  

We’re sending a narrow topical signal (“heating repair”) where we need a broader local signal (“HVAC services in [City]”). Assuming the client wants to rank for HVAC and not just Heating

My suggestion:

1. URL restructure to /[city]/hvac-services/

2.  Change the default headings and copy to reflect HVAC coverage
Current:  “Arco Comfort Air provides expert heating installation and repair in Cleveland.” 
Proposed:  “Arco Comfort Air provides full-service HVAC installation and repair in Cleveland, including heating, cooling, and air quality solutions.” 

3.   Add service category sections: 
- Heating Services
- Cooling Services
- Plumbing
- Electrical

Before we consider this, I would like to hear if the client is intentionally trying to rank locally for Heating only. If yes, we leave it alone.

 could you kindly assist in assigning to the correct person that can give me more insight on this :)
